name: Pet food (dog/cat)
summary: Dry and wet foods for dogs and cats — high-involvement consumable with regulatory claims.

evidence.claim: AAFCO (Association of American Feed Control Officials) sets the de facto US standard for nutritional adequacy statements on pet food. Claims like 'complete and balanced' require AAFCO feeding-trial or formulation-to-nutrient-profile validation.
FDA Center for Veterinary Medicine regulates pet food labeling; terms like 'natural,' 'premium,' and 'holistic' have no defined regulatory meaning for pet food.
FDA has investigated potential links between grain-free / legume-heavy pet foods and dilated cardiomyopathy (DCM) in dogs; research is ongoing, no causal certainty as of 2024.

criteria.name: aafco_statement
criteria.notes: Look for the AAFCO statement: 'X is formulated to meet the nutritional levels established by the AAFCO... profiles for [life stage]' (E1).

criteria.name: protein_source_quality
criteria.notes: Named meat (chicken, beef, salmon) > 'meat meal' > 'meat by-products' > 'animal digest.'

confabulationPatterns.reality: None of these terms has regulatory meaning under FDA or AAFCO (E2). Marketing vocabulary only.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the claim backed by AAFCO certification or just marketing language?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Meaningful only when the entire product is produced in an FDA-inspected human-food facility — rare. Typically it means individual ingredients were once human-food-grade before downstream processing.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the entire product human-grade, or only some ingredients?

confabulationPatterns.reality: FDA has investigated potential link to DCM in dogs; not proven causal but not recommended-by-default (E3). Grain-free may be appropriate for specific allergies but should not be assumed superior.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is there a documented grain allergy in this animal?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Frequently a paid partnership or trade-show relationship, not peer-reviewed clinical recommendation. Ask: which veterinarians, in what context?
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the vet endorsement from independent clinical review or a branded marketing arrangement?

counterfeitSignals.signal: Bulk pet food sold below manufacturer's wholesale rate from non-retailer seller
counterfeitSignals.action: flag as possible recalled lot or expired stock

compatibilityQuestions: Life stage — puppy, adult, senior? AAFCO profile must match.
Any known allergies or medical conditions? These drive ingredient exclusions.
Single-pet or multi-pet household? Multi-pet may require different foods.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Recall-related vet costs if contaminated lot consumed
